steps.title: Use the cone volume formula
steps.content: Use the given diameter to find the radius, then substitute the radius and height into the cone volume formula.

steps.title: Extract the radius and state the cone volume formula
steps.content: Given: height h = 22 cm and base diameter = 6 cm. The radius is half the diameter: r = 3 cm. Use the cone volume formula: $$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h$$

steps.title: Substitute and simplify to find n
steps.content:
Substitute $r = 3$ and $h = 22$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi (3)^2 (22)$$
Simplify $(3)^2 = 9$:
$$V = \frac{1}{3}\pi \cdot 9 \cdot 22$$
Simplify $\frac{9}{3} = 3$:
$$V = 3 \cdot 22 \cdot \pi = 66\pi$$
Since the volume is expressed as $n\pi$ cm³, n = 66.
